{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":321681,"duration_api_ms":321865,"ttft_ms":4086,"num_turns":41,"result":"Review complete. **Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** — CP00-374 does not block pack or goal closeout.

The descriptor-only billing pack honestly reports `production_ready: false` (pending this review + human approval), and every boundary that matters is enforced in code, tests, the contract, and the RP12 validator:
- 150 units with deliverable/micro-title distributions matching baseline, registry, manifest, and validator exactly
- No-write / no-real-data / no-Hermes-runtime guards all `false`/`true` as required
- Claude held as read-only, non-final-approver; Hermes used only for deterministic evidence packets
- CP178+ hardening satisfied (forbidden-evidence list, non-zero auth preflight, prompt within size limit, no forbidden tools)
- Changed-file scope clean; unrelated dirty files correctly excluded

Only one **P3** (by-design, non-blocking): the cumulative `no_write_attestation` carries a CP364-era label under the CP00-374 `pack_id` via the spread chain — drift-guarded by the validator, purely cosmetic. No P0/P1/P2 findings.","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"e972e5e4-f415-4faf-83d7-b34c1228e50f","total_cost_usd":2.36395475,"usage":{"input_tokens":1172,"cache_creation_input_tokens":128139,"cache_read_input_tokens":1950490,"output_tokens":23229,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":128139,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":2,"output_tokens":445,"cache_read_input_tokens":127888,"cache_creation_input_tokens":8646,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":8646},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"modelUsage":{"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001":{"inputTokens":1151,"outputTokens":21,"cacheReadInputTokens":0,"cacheCreationInputTokens":0,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":0.001256,"contextWindow":200000,"maxOutputTokens":32000},"claude-opus-4-8":{"inputTokens":1172,"outputTokens":23229,"cacheReadInputTokens":1950490,"cacheCreationInputTokens":128139,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":2.3626987499999994,"contextWindow":200000,"maxOutputTokens":32000}},"permission_denials":[],"structured_output":{"overall_verdict":"PASS_WITH_FINDINGS","blocks_pack_closeout":false,"blocks_goal_closeout":false,"no_unresolved_p0":true,"no_unresolved_p1":true,"summary":"CP00-374 is a descriptor-only billing-core pack (RP12.P03.M06.S13-RP12.P04.M03.S20, 150 units) that closes RP12.P03 and opens the RP12.P04 foundation as nine descriptor-only sections. The pack-level claim is honestly production_ready=false / status=implemented_pending_review, supported by implementation (service.js/registry.js), tests (3 dedicated CP374 tests in model.test.js), the contract projection, and the RP12 validator — all of which enforce descriptor-only, no-write (writes_product_state=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), and no-real-data (no_real_data=true, synthetic_only=true) boundaries. Unit count, deliverable distribution, and micro-title distribution match exactly across the prompt baseline, registry requirements, manifest, and validate script (sum=150). Claude is correctly held as a read-only, non-final-approver reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob), and Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors, never product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failure, 0-byte output, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields; readiness auth preflight passed with non-zero stdout; prompt is within the 25600B limit; forbidden tools absent; summary-only correctly prohibited for Risk C. Changed-file scope is clean — all dirty files are within active implementation refs and unrelated dirty files are correctly excluded. One non-blocking P3 readability observation; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Does not block pack or goal closeout; human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust or runtime opening.","findings":[{"id":"CP00-374-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"Cumulative no_write_attestation retains CP364-era label under CP00-374 pack_id","file":"packages/billing/src/registry.js","line":1048,"details":"BILLING_CORE_CP374_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is built by spreading the CP373 attestation (which transitively spreads down to the CP364 base) and overriding pack_id to CP00-374 plus adding validates_p03_closeout_p04_foundation_descriptor_only:true. As a result the CP374 attestation (and the mirrored contracts/billing-core-contract.json top-level no_write_attestation at lines 2211-2241) still carries the original CP364 field validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only:true under pack_id CP00-374. This is intentional cumulative attestation and is strictly drift-guarded by the validator's assert.deepEqual against the registry constant, so there is no correctness or boundary risk; it is purely a readability nuance for a future reader scanning the contract who may momentarily expect a CP364-named field not to appear under a CP374 pack_id.","recommendation":"No action required for closeout. Optionally, a future cleanup could add a short comment documenting the cumulative-attestation spread chain, or rename the accumulated descriptor-validation flags to a single list, to make the intent self-evident.","post_review_disposition":"Acknowledged as by-design cumulative attestation; non-blocking; no change required for CP00-374 closeout."}],"finding_counts":{"p0":0,"p1":0,"p2":0,"p3":1,"reported_p0":0,"reported_p1":0,"reported_p2":0,"reported_p3":1}},"terminal_reason":"completed","fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"361732a7-4db4-4984-a2c5-228b5542957d"}
